title: porophyllum ruderale
intro: Porophyllum ruderale, also known as Mexican tea or cilantro leaves, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its leaves, when consumed, have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Porophyllum ruderale can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water or using it as a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
